title: Enterprise AI/AGI Governance Framework for Large Financial & Fortune 500 Enterprises (2026-2030)
classification: Confidential / Restricted — Board, CRO, CCO, CISO, CDAO, Group Internal Audit, External Regulators (on request)

directive.scope: End-to-end enterprise AI/AGI governance operating model for Fortune 500 / Global 2000 / G-SIFIs spanning policy, control, risk, compliance, security, model risk, third-party, AGI containment, and AI Governance Hub architecture
outcomes: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 certified AIMS by 2027 across all material AI systems
NIST AI RMF 1.0 + AI 600-1 generative profile mapped to >=95% of in-scope models
EU AI Act 2026 Article 6/9/10/14/15 + GPAI 53/55 obligations fully evidenced
GDPR DPIA + Article 22 + FCRA/ECOA adverse-action automation in production
Basel III/IV + SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12 model risk lifecycle managed in single MRM platform
FCA Consumer Duty + SMCR SMF-attested AI accountability operating model
MAS FEAT + HKMA GP-1/GS-2 fairness, ethics, accountability, transparency in production
Kafka audit log with WORM + PQC sealing across all model decisions
Kubernetes + container security with policy-as-code (OPA/Rego) at admission and runtime
AGI containment T0-T4 with 3-of-5 quorum + kinetic override and AI Safety Institute notifications
doNot: Do NOT deploy any AI/AGI capability without Enterprise AI Governance Hub registration, ISO 42001 risk assessment, and model risk tier classification
Do NOT bypass Kafka audit logging, OPA/Rego policy gates, WORM/PQC sealing, or 3-of-5 frontier quorum

tiers.T0: Sandbox - isolated VPC, synthetic data only, no production traffic
tiers.T1: Staging - shadow mode, real data, no customer impact
tiers.T3: Production - Nitro Enclaves + KMS + dual control + full audit
tiers.T4: Frontier Air-Gapped - 3-of-5 quorum + kinetic override + AI Safety Institute notification

severities.SEV-0: Civilizational/systemic - EU AI Office notification <=15d, AISI notification <=24h

modules.title: ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS + NIST AI RMF + OECD + EU AI Act Foundation
modules.summary: Integrated AI Management System anchored on ISO/IEC 42001:2023 with NIST AI RMF 1.0 functions (Govern/Map/Measure/Manage), OECD AI Principles, and EU AI Act 2024/1689 Article 6/9/10/14/15 + GPAI 53/55 mappings.

sections.title: ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS Architecture
sections.policyCommit: Board-attested AI Policy, AI Objectives, AI Risk Appetite Statement signed annually by Group CEO + Group CRO
sections.scope: All AI/AGI systems with material impact on customers, employees, regulators, capital, or systemic risk
sections.structure: AIMS clauses 4-10 mapped to enterprise functions: Context (Strategy), Leadership (CDAO+CRO), Planning (PMO), Support (HR+Procurement+IT), Operation (Lines of Business), Performance (Internal Audit), Improvement (CCO)

sections.title: NIST AI RMF 1.0 + AI 600-1 Generative Profile
sections.generativeProfile: NIST AI 600-1 mapped to all FM/LLM use cases with synthetic content provenance (C2PA)
sections.crossWalk: Bidirectional crosswalk ISO 42001 <-> NIST AI RMF <-> EU AI Act maintained in MRM platform

sections.title: OECD AI Principles 2019/2024 Implementation
sections.implementation: OECD-aligned model cards + system cards published for all T2+ systems with public-facing summary

sections.title: EU AI Act 2024/1689 Compliance Layer
gpai: Art. 55 systemic-risk: evaluations, adversarial testing, cybersecurity, incident reporting
sections.timeline: Prohibited practices Feb 2025; GPAI Aug 2025; high-risk Aug 2026

modules.title: Financial-Services Model Risk Management (SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 / Basel III/IV / ICAAP)
modules.summary: Three-lines-of-defense model risk operating model with SR 11-7 conceptual soundness, ongoing monitoring, outcomes analysis; OCC 2011-12 effective challenge; Basel III/IV IRB/IMA model validation; ICAAP integration with Pillar 2.

sections.title: Model Risk Lifecycle
sections.tiering: Tier-1 (regulatory capital, P&L, capital plan) / Tier-2 (material business) / Tier-3 (limited scope) / Tier-4 (research)
sections.cadence: Tier-1 annual validation; Tier-2 biennial; Tier-3 every 3y; ongoing monitoring monthly for all

sections.title: SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12 Effective Challenge
sections.conceptualSoundness: Independent review of theory, assumptions, design choices
sections.outcomesAnalysis: Champion/challenger + counterfactual analysis on production decisions

sections.title: Basel III/IV IRB/IMA + FRTB
sections.validation: Independent validation per SR 15-19/SR 15-18; quantitative review every cycle
sections.capitalImpact: MRM platform feeds Pillar 2 model risk capital add-on into ICAAP

sections.title: AI/ML-Specific MRM Extensions
extensions: Concept drift + data drift monitoring (PSI, KS, KL, Wasserstein)
Adversarial robustness testing (PGD, BIM, NLP attacks)
Provenance of training data + lineage to feature store

sections.title: ICAAP / Pillar 2 Integration
sections.integration: Aggregate model risk capital + AI-specific add-ons fed into ICAAP Pillar 2 alongside operational, reputational, strategic risk
sections.governance: MRC quarterly review of capital adequacy; Board-attested annual ICAAP includes AI risk section

modules.title: Data Protection & Consumer Fairness (GDPR / FCRA / ECOA / FCA Consumer Duty)
modules.summary: Privacy-by-design with GDPR Article 22 (automated decisions), FCRA adverse-action notices, ECOA Reg-B disparate impact testing, FCA Consumer Duty cross-cutting rules + four outcomes; MAS FEAT + HKMA GP-1 fairness operationalization.

sections.title: GDPR Article 22 + DPIA Operationalization
sections.dpia: DPIA required for all T2+ models processing personal data; reviewed by DPO + Group Legal

article22.prohibition: No solely automated decisions producing legal/significant effects without explicit consent or necessity
article22.logging: All Art-22 invocations logged to Kafka audit topic

sections.crossBorder: EU SCC + UK IDTA + adequacy assessments documented in ROPA

sections.title: FCRA + ECOA Reg-B Adverse-Action Automation
sections.adverseAction: Automated FCRA 615(a) + ECOA Reg-B section 1002.9 notices generated within 30 days of adverse decision
sections.reasonCodes: Top-N reason codes derived from SHAP attribution, mapped to plain-English statements vetted by Compliance Legal
sections.disparateImpact: Quarterly disparate-impact analysis on credit, hiring, insurance models (race, sex, age, national origin)

sections.title: FCA Consumer Duty + Cross-Cutting Rules
crossCutting: Enable customers to pursue financial objectives
sections.evidence: Consumer Duty Board Report annual; AI-driven personalization included with foreseeable-harm assessment
sections.vulnerableCustomers: Vulnerable-customer flag piped to AI systems; fairness uplift required where applicable

sections.title: MAS FEAT + HKMA GP-1 / GS-2
sections.hkmaGP1: Governance of AI applications in banking — board-level accountability, risk management, fair treatment
sections.hkmaGS2: Generative AI applications guidance — data governance, model governance, human oversight, cybersecurity

sections.title: Privacy-Enhancing Technologies (PETs)
pets: Confidential computing (AMD SEV-SNP, Intel TDX, AWS Nitro)
sections.policy: PETs mandated for cross-border training and any T3 model handling special category data

modules.title: Kafka-Based Audit Logging + WORM Storage + Post-Quantum Cryptography
modules.summary: Enterprise-wide tamper-evident audit log over Apache Kafka with WORM (S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE / Azure Immutable / GCS Bucket Lock) and PQC-signed seals (ML-DSA / ML-KEM / SLH-DSA) per NIST FIPS 203/204/205.

sections.title: Kafka Audit Topic Architecture
sections.retention: Hot 90d in Kafka tiered storage; cold WORM 7-25y per regime (SEC 17a-4 7y, GDPR varies, FINRA 6y, DORA 5y)
sections.partitioning: By LOB + decisionId; replication factor 3 across AZs; minISR=2; producer acks=all

sections.title: Tamper-Evident Sealing
sections.chain: Each record hashed (SHA-3-512); merkle-tree aggregated per minute; root signed with ML-DSA-87 (FIPS 204) + SLH-DSA fallback
sections.anchoring: Daily merkle root anchored to QLDB + external timestamp authority (TSA RFC 3161) + optional public chain
sections.verification: Independent verifier service replays Kafka offsets and recomputes merkle roots on demand

sections.title: WORM Storage Tier
backends: AWS S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode
On-prem Dell ECS / NetApp SnapLock Compliance
sections.policy: Legal-hold + retention-lock dual control; no delete path even for root accounts; SEC 17a-4(f) WORM attestation by independent third party

sections.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Stack
sections.hybrid: Hybrid TLS classical+PQ during transition (X25519+ML-KEM-768) per NSA CNSA 2.0 timeline 2025-2033
sections.keyMgmt: HSM-backed (AWS CloudHSM / Azure Dedicated HSM / Thales Luna 7) with FIPS 140-3 Level 3

sections.title: Audit Query + Regulator Access
sections.queryLayer: ksqlDB + Trino over Iceberg on WORM; row-level filters per LOB + regulator scope
sections.regulatorPortal: Read-only portal for EU AI Office, FCA, MAS, HKMA, SEC, FINRA with audit-of-audit logging

modules.title: Container & Kubernetes Security for AI Workloads
modules.summary: Defense-in-depth for AI model serving on Kubernetes spanning image supply chain (SLSA L4), admission control, runtime security, network policy, secrets, and confidential containers.

sections.title: Image Supply Chain (SLSA L4 / SSDF)
sections.policyGate: Kyverno/OPA admission rejects unsigned, unscanned, or non-policy-compliant images

sections.title: Admission Control + Pod Security
sections.psa: Pod Security Admission 'restricted' profile cluster-wide for AI namespaces

sections.title: Runtime Security
sections.response: Auto-isolation + Kafka aigov.containment-events on anomaly; SRE+SecOps page within 5 min

sections.title: Network Policy + Service Mesh
sections.netpol: Default-deny Cilium NetworkPolicy + L7 HTTP/gRPC filtering
sections.mesh: Istio/Linkerd mTLS for service-to-service; SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identities
sections.egress: Per-namespace egress allowlist with FQDN policies; DNS over HTTPS

sections.title: Confidential Containers + Secrets
sections.confidential: Confidential containers (CoCo) on AMD SEV-SNP / Intel TDX / AWS Nitro Enclaves for T3/T4 workloads
secrets: AWS Secrets Manager / Azure Key Vault for cloud
sections.kms: Per-tenant KMS keys; envelope encryption; key rotation 90d

modules.title: Policy-as-Code with OPA/Rego
modules.summary: Unified policy plane using OPA/Rego for admission control, runtime authorization, data access, model deployment gates, and regulator-facing evidence. Includes Conftest CI, OPAL bundle distribution, and decision logging to Kafka.

sections.title: OPA/Rego Policy Architecture
sections.distribution: OPAL pulls bundles from Git; signed bundles via Cosign; rollout via GitOps (Argo CD)

sections.title: Model Deployment Gates
gates: EU AI Act risk class declared
FCRA/ECOA fairness report attached for credit models
sections.failureMode: Deployment blocked at Argo CD; aigov.policy-changes Kafka topic records denial with rationale

sections.title: Runtime Authorization
sections.envoy: ext_authz to OPA sidecar; sub-ms decision latency; cached with TTL
sections.denyLog: All deny decisions Kafka aigov.access; sample of allow decisions for spot-audit

sections.title: Data Access + Purpose Limitation
sections.policy: GDPR purpose-limitation enforced as Rego: each query must declare purposeId from approved catalog; mismatch = deny
sections.piiPolicy: PII columns tagged via data catalog (Atlan/Collibra); OPA enforces masking/tokenization based on consumer role + purpose
sections.crossBorder: Rego enforces data-residency: EU PII can only flow to EU compute; logged + alertable

sections.title: Regulator Evidence Generation
sections.evidence: OPA decision log + bundle signatures + Git history produce regulator-grade evidence pack on demand
sections.attestations: Quarterly attestations to EU AI Office, FCA, MAS, HKMA, SEC with OPA decision log extracts
sections.opaBundleHash: Bundle SHA-256 + ML-DSA signature pinned per deployment generation

modules.title: AI Red-Teaming Program (Frontier + Production)
modules.summary: Continuous adversarial evaluation across pre-deployment, deployment, and post-deployment phases covering jailbreaks, prompt injection, data exfiltration, model extraction, poisoning, evasion, fairness probes, and AGI/ASI capability elicitation.

sections.title: Red-Team Operating Model
sections.structure: Internal red team (10-25 FTE) + external firms (e.g., Trail of Bits, NCC, Bishop Fox) + crowdsourced (HackerOne private programs)
sections.governance: Reports to CISO + CDAO; independent of MRM and engineering; quarterly board readout
scope: GPAI frontier models monthly per EU AI Act Art. 55

sections.title: Attack Taxonomy
taxonomy: Data exfiltration (training data extraction, memorization probes)

sections.title: Evaluations Suite
evals: ARC Evals dangerous-capability suite for frontier
Custom domain-specific evals for finance / health / legal
sections.cadence: Pre-deployment full suite; monthly drift for T2+; weekly for T3/T4

sections.title: AGI Capability Elicitation
sections.method: Apollo Research / METR-style dangerous-capability evals: persuasion, deception, self-replication, autonomous coding, weapons-of-mass-destruction uplift, cyber offense, AI R&D
sections.triggers: Capability score crossing predefined thresholds triggers SEV-0 review + AISI notification within 24h
sections.mitigation: Capability suppression via fine-tuning / RLHF / circuit-level interventions; deployment hold pending Board review

sections.title: Reporting + Remediation
sections.reporting: Standardized red-team report: findings, CVSS-style severity, exploitability, business impact, remediation plan
sections.evidence: All findings + remediation in Kafka aigov.policy-changes + linked to MRM platform issue tracker

modules.title: AGI / ASI Containment Strategies
modules.summary: Tiered containment T0-T4 with 3-of-5 human quorum, kinetic override, formally-verified safety properties, and AI Safety Institute coordination for frontier systems approaching AGI/ASI thresholds.

tiers.T0: Sandbox - hermetic VPC, synthetic data, no network egress
tiers.T1: Staging - shadow mode behind T2; real data; no actuation
tiers.T2: Canary - <=1% traffic; auto-rollback on KPI breach
tiers.T3: Production - Nitro Enclaves / TDX; dual-control deploy; full audit
tiers.T4: Frontier Air-Gapped - 3-of-5 quorum (CRO+CISO+CDAO+Board AI Chair+External AISI rep); kinetic override (physical power cutoff); AISI notification <=24h

sections.title: Frontier 3-of-5 Quorum + Kinetic Override
sections.quorum: Any T4 deployment, scaling, capability enable, or weights export requires 3-of-5 signed approvals via hardware tokens (FIDO2 + ML-DSA)
sections.kinetic: Physical kill switches at PDU level + smart-PDU API; tested quarterly with full power-off drill
sections.timeLock: 48h time-lock between approval and execution allows external review

sections.title: Formally-Verified Safety Properties
properties: No-egress invariant (network namespace cannot bind external)
Compute budget invariant (cgroup CPU/GPU caps signed)
Capability ceiling (evals must remain below thresholds)
sections.verification: TLA+ specs for control plane; Lean/Coq proofs for critical invariants; runtime enforcement via eBPF + LSM

sections.title: AISI / Regulator Coordination
notifications: Pre-training run >10^25 FLOPs (EU AI Act Art. 51)
sections.mou: Bilateral MoUs with UK AISI + US AISI + EU AI Office for evals access + incident sharing

sections.title: Containment Failure Response
playbook: Detect: runtime anomaly / capability threshold / unauthorized action
Isolate: cilium network policy to drop, scale to 0, freeze weights
Notify: SEV-0 paged to CRO+CISO+CDAO+Board AI Chair+AISI
Investigate: forensic snapshot, immutable image, root cause analysis
Communicate: regulator notifications EU AI Office <=15d, SEC 8-K <=4 BD if material
Recover: only after 3-of-5 quorum + external review sign-off

modules.title: Enterprise AI Governance Hub Architecture
modules.summary: Single pane of glass integrating model inventory, MRM, risk register, policy catalog, evidence pack, regulator portal, decision logs, AGI watchtower, and red-team findings. Built on event-sourced + GraphQL + OIDC + WORM-backed.

personas.CDAO: Strategic dashboards, AI portfolio risk, value vs risk
personas.CRO: Risk appetite vs actual, model risk capital, top-10 risks
personas.CCO: Regulator queries, evidence pack generation, attestations
personas.CISO: Red-team findings, runtime anomalies, containment events
personas.Regulator: Read-only portal, evidence pack, decision log queries

sections.title: Deployment + Operations
sections.deployment: Multi-region active-active on EKS/GKE/AKS + on-prem OpenShift; Argo CD GitOps; Terraform + Crossplane
sections.cost: USD 25-60M / 5y TCO including platform + 80-150 FTE governance staff

policies.statement: Board-attested AI Policy aligned to ISO/IEC 42001 clauses 4-10
AI Risk Appetite Statement covering model, ethical, regulatory, AGI risks
Acceptable Use Policy for generative AI including data classification rules
Model Risk Management Policy per SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12
AI Data Governance Policy with purpose-limitation + minimization
AI Privacy Policy per GDPR Art. 22 + UK DPA
AI Fairness Policy per FCRA/ECOA + MAS FEAT + HKMA GP-1
AI Third-Party Risk Policy per DORA + EBA Outsourcing
AI Red-Teaming Policy per EU AI Act Art. 55 + NIST AI 600-1
AI Incident Response Policy per DORA + SEC Cyber Rules
AI Audit Logging Policy per SEC 17a-4 + FINRA 4511
PQC Cryptography Policy per NSA CNSA 2.0 + NIST FIPS 203/204/205
Generative AI Governance Policy per EU AI Act GPAI Art. 53/55 + NIST AI 600-1

controls.control: AI Risk Register integrated with ERM
Training data lineage to feature store
Model card + system card per OECD + EU AI Act

k8sControls.mechanism: Cilium L7 HTTP/gRPC filter for egress

opaPolicies.description: Reject pod if image not Cosign-signed
Require DPIA if data class includes PII
Require EU AI Act risk class declaration
Require fairness report for credit models
GDPR Art. 5 purpose-limitation check on queries
EU PII must remain on EU compute
FCA Consumer Duty uplift for vulnerable cust
Block deploy if capability evals breach thresholds

wormControls.mechanism: Dell ECS Compliance / NetApp SnapLock Compliance
Daily merkle root to QLDB + RFC 3161 TSA
AWS CloudHSM / Azure Dedicated HSM / Thales Luna 7
Hybrid TLS X25519 + ML-KEM-768 per NSA CNSA 2.0

redTeams.technique: Backdoor trigger injection in fine-tune data

agiContainments.mechanism: Staging shadow, real data, no actuation
FIDO2 + ML-DSA hardware tokens for 5 approvers
PDU-level smart power cutoff API + manual
Compute budget cgroup CPU/GPU caps signed
Capability ceiling evals must stay below thresholds
TLA+ spec for control plane safety

hubComponents.component: REST adapter for legacy GRC tools

code.name: falco/rules-ai.yaml
code.purpose: Falco rules for AI workload anomalies

code.name: redteam/prompt_injection.py
code.purpose: Prompt injection harness with OWASP LLM01 vectors

code.name: specs/control_plane.tla
code.purpose: TLA+ spec for AGI control plane invariants

code.name: argo-cd/aigov-app.yaml
code.purpose: Argo CD GitOps app for Hub

kpis.name: EU AI Office notifications on time
DORA major incident reports on time

privacy.dpiaPolicy: Required for all T2+ models with PII or special category
privacy.minimization: Purpose-limitation enforced via OPA-04..09; data minimization audited annually

tiering: T0 sandbox -> T1 staging -> T2 canary <=1% -> T3 prod Nitro Enclaves -> T4 frontier air-gapped
deployment.gitops: Argo CD + Crossplane + Terraform; signed manifests; environment promotion via PR

deliverables: Kafka audit topics aigov.decisions + policy-changes live
Red-team baseline run on top-10 T2 models
OPA gates in prod for T2+
First quarterly Board AI Risk Cmt

roadmap.milestone: Hub GA; ISO 42001 stage-1 audit; OPA enterprise rollout; first GPAI Art. 55 attestation
ISO 42001 certified; full EU AI Act high-risk coverage; PQC ML-DSA on all seals; FCA Consumer Duty embedded
Full Kafka+WORM regulator portals; T4 frontier evals operationalized; AISI MoUs active; PQC >=80%
Federated PETs + confidential containers default for T3; cross-border data residency 100% OPA-enforced
PQC 100% across all sealing + TLS; AGI containment T4 industrialized; Hub federation across G-SIFI peers

executiveSummary.thesis: Enterprise AI/AGI governance for Fortune 500 / Global 2000 / G-SIFIs requires an integrated operating model anchored on ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS, mapped bidirectionally to NIST AI RMF + EU AI Act + GDPR + sectoral financial regimes (SR 11-7, Basel, FCA Consumer Duty, MAS FEAT, HKMA GP-1/GS-2), operationalized via Kafka audit + WORM + PQC + Kubernetes security + OPA/Rego + MRM platform + red-teaming + AGI containment T0-T4, surfaced through a single AI Governance Hub.
